name: qsv-scoresql
description: Analyze a SQL query against CSV file caches (stats, moarstats, frequency) to produce a performance score with actionable optimization suggestions BEFORE running the query. Supports Polars (default) and DuckDB modes.

options.description: The field delimiter for reading CSV data. Must be a single character.
Use DuckDB for query plan analysis instead of Polars. Uses the QSV_DUCKDB_PATH environment variable if set, otherwise looks for "duckdb" in PATH.
Number of rows to scan when inferring schema.
Output results as JSON instead of human-readable report.
Write output to <file> instead of stdout.
Truncate lines with more fields than the header.
Automatically try to parse dates/datetimes and time.

examples.description: Score a simple filter query against a single CSV file
examples.command: qsv scoresql data.csv "SELECT * FROM data WHERE col1 > 10"

examples.description: Output the score report as JSON instead of the default human-readable format
examples.command: qsv scoresql --json data.csv "SELECT col1, col2 FROM data ORDER BY col1"

examples.description: Score a join query across two CSV files
examples.command: qsv scoresql data.csv data2.csv "SELECT * FROM data JOIN data2 ON data.id = data2.id"

examples.description: Use DuckDB for query plan analysis instead of Polars
examples.command: qsv scoresql --duckdb data.csv "SELECT * FROM data WHERE status = 'active'"

examples.description: Use _t_N aliases just like sqlp (see sqlp documentation)
examples.command: qsv scoresql data.csv data2.csv "SELECT * FROM _t_1 JOIN _t_2 ON _t_1.id = _t_2.id"

examples.description: Score a query from a SQL script file (only the last query is scored)
